claimText: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded for the development of Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs).
evidenceExcerpt: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded for the development of Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs).
examUse: Statement-based question on recent Nobel Prizes in science.

claimText: Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs) are composed of metal ions acting as joints and organic carbon-based molecules acting as links.
evidenceExcerpt: MOFs are special materials made of metal ions (acting as joints) and organic carbon-based molecules (acting as links).
examUse: Statement-based question on the structural composition of MOFs.
distractorHooks: Organic molecules act as joints and metal ions as links
Made of polymers without metal ions

claimText: Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs) are highly porous materials that form 1D, 2D, or 3D network structures containing cavities.
evidenceExcerpt: They join through chemical bonds to form 1D, 2D, or 3D network structures. These structures contain cavities, making the material highly porous.
examUse: Feature-based question on the physical properties of MOFs.
distractorHooks: MOFs are highly dense and non-porous
